sections.subject: Literature

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content:
This course includes the following topic(s): Lit./Mythology: Grade 3, General Lit.: Grade 3, Community Read Alouds: Grades 1-12

About Community Read Alouds: Grades 1-12
This is meant to be a read-aloud for all ages to enjoy together in the afternoon at least 1 time a week. We encourage you to choose a book that is specific to your region. However, we will offer some suggestions in the Grades 1-3 Literature lessons and Grades 4+ Citizenship lessons. The suggested book titles will be available June 1st in the lesson plans.

About General Lit.: Grade 3
Accessible texts such as Charlotte's Web help students grow the delight of reading for themselves.

About Lit./Mythology: Grade 3
Continue to expose students to rich stories with either Pilgrim's Progress or classic Greek myths.

entries.title: Placement & Combining Tips
entries.content:
General Lit.: Grade 3
Selections in General Literature are chosen to allow students to begin taking turns reading with their teacher and/or entirely by themselves (depending on reading level). If combining with younger students, make sure emerging readers still have space to practice reading somewhere in their lessons.

Lit./Mythology: Grade 3
Lit/Mythology selections are continued from Grade 2. Students can jump in at the current rotation or use Grade 2 lessons if you prefer to start from the beginning. Students in Grades 1-3 can be combined for these lessons.

rows.label: Community Read Alouds: Grades 1-12
rows.scheduleInfo:
Community Read Alouds: Grades 1-12
1+ time/week 20 min+

rows.label: Lit./Mythology: Grade 3
rows.books: A Wonder Book for Girls and Boys||Pilgrim's Progress: Updated, Modern English

entries.content:
Permission to print for non-commercial use. See Alveary group use policy to use lessons
in a group context.

LINKS: Click text or scan the QR code in the top corner of the lesson plan pages to view
online resources associated with the lessons.

Responsibility for previewing all links rests with the teacher. All links were checked at the
time of publication; however, websites change frequently and may contain objectionable
content. Please report broken links by contacting us through our website.

Literature: Grade 3
☐ Grade 3 should write a narration every couple of weeks from the term's reading, or write a part and tell the rest. The video "Tips for Transitioning from Oral to Written Narration" can be found in the Quick Links. As indicated in the video, students who are not yet secure with penmanship are not yet ready for written narration. In these cases, students may continue with oral and/or illustrated narrations only. Continue penmanship, but instead of written narrations, use the time to have students work on skills that will better support their development, such as climbing on trees or playground equipment, doing household chores, working clay or dough, and doing mazes and dot-to-dot activities.

☐ As desired, plan for a read-aloud time. Suggestions are provided in the Community Read Aloud Quick Link, but local favorites are highly recommended whenever possible.

books.title: A Wonder Book for Girls and Boys

links.label: Transitioning from Oral to Written Narration

blocks.text:
• Read through the lesson and gather any materials or find any necessary links. 

• If you have not already read the book, you may want to read the selected pages in advance.

• Connect back to the previous lesson. Ask students to give a summary of what they remember. (Full narration is not necessary.)

• If students have difficulty recalling, teachers might try showing them a picture from the reading, sharing something that the teacher remembers, or prompting them with the first sentence of the last reading. This will often refresh their memory enough to allow them to recall.

• If students still cannot recall anything from the last reading, then teachers might consider continuing anyway (with a shorter reading this time) or repeating the last lesson, depending on the reading and the nature of the book.

• Optional: use a picture, map, or engaging question to pique the student's interest in the day's lesson. 

• If necessary, you may talk about one or two unfamiliar words that will be crucial to understanding.

• Read the day's passage out loud. When reading to pre-readers, point to the words as you read, even if it seems like they aren't paying attention.

• Move at the student’s pace for the lesson time rather than a particular number of pages. It is vitally important that teachers not try to ‘force feed’ a student more than they can consume in one sitting.

• Students who have a difficult time sitting still or listening may benefit from a fidget of their choice, such as a piece of putty or clay or an afghan; flexible positioning, such as a wobble seat or inverted sitting; or sensory modulation, such as a weighted vest or blanket or adjustment of light, temperature, or sound.

• Have students retell the passage orally.

• Alternatively, learners may use words, pictures, Legos, dolls, etc., to process and convey ideas in the manner most natural to them.  

• Teachers may take turns to model.

• Narration is a complex skill requiring the coordination of several parts of the brain. If a student struggles to narrate in general, with a specific subject or book, or on one day but not others, then this is an opportunity to learn. Observe when they struggle with this or any other activity today. Notice facial expressions and body language; listen to what they say about why it is hard or how they feel about it. Take note of these observations for later, tell them that you see how hard they are working, and then offer whatever support they need to end on a positive note. If necessary, take a break for the day.

• Give the student an opportunity to share thoughts on the lesson and any connections made.  

• To stimulate discussion, you could ask them what they thought about the passage or ask an open-ended question.

lessons.title: 15m General Lit.: Grade 3 - Lesson 1
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: Charlotte's Web

➜ INTRO
Look at the cover of the book. Can you guess what this story will be about? Let's see if you're right!

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.I

• What did you think of what happened?

lessons.title: 15m Lit./Mythology: Grade 3 - Lesson 1
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: A Wonder Book OR Pilgrim's Progress Book 2

OPTION 1: A Wonder Book
PREP: Read A Wonder Book for Girls and Boys
"Preface" 

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS (Option 1: A Wonder Book)
"Introductory to The Three Golden Apples" p.110-112 "The snow-storm" - "made their appearance."
---------------
OPTION 2: Pilgrim's Progress Book 2
➜ INTRO
In Book 1 of Pilgrim's Progress, Christian went on a journey from the City of Destruction to the Celestial City, but his wife Christiana and their children would not go with him. Book 2 is the story of Christiana and the children. As the story begins, the narrator is talking to Mr. Sagacity. "Sagacity" means great intelligence and wisdom.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"Introduction" p.197-202 "Courteous companions" - "his companions."
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER TIP
Be sure to watch the video in the Quick Links and take note of Planning and Prep before beginning any written narration!

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS (Option 2: Pilgrim's Progress Book 2)
"Introduction" p.203-209 "The next morning" - "handsome fellow."

Be sure to watch the video in the Quick Links and take note of Planning and Prep before beginning any written narration!

lessons.title: 15m Lit./Mythology: Grade 3 - Lesson 26
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: A Wonder Book OR Pilgrim's Progress Book 2

➜ RECAP
Where did we end last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS (Option 1: A Wonder Book)
"The Chimaera" p.190-193 "Once, in the" - "storm lasted."
---------------
➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS (Option 2: Pilgrim's Progress Book 2)
"The Sixth Stage" p.301-305 "Great Heart said" - "used to do."
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.body:

➜ RECAP

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS (Option 1: A Wonder Book)

---------------
➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS (Option 2: Pilgrim's Progress Book 2)

---------------
➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS (Option 2: Pilgrim's Progress Book 2)

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS (Option 2: Pilgrim's Progress Book 2)
"The Eighth Stage" p.337-342 "They went through "- "heel or another."

---------------
➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS (Option 2: Pilgrim's Progress Book 2)
"The Eighth Stage" p.343-348 - "They all commend" - "to their place."

lessons.title: 15m Lit./Mythology: Grade 3 - Lesson 35
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: A Wonder Book OR Pilgrim's Progress Book 2

➜ CATCH UP (Option 1: A Wonder Book)
Use this time to catch up on lessons in this or another course, or explore Extra Helpings.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS (Option 2: Pilgrim's Progress Book 2)
"The Eighth Stage" p.348 - 352 "Christiana called at the gate" - "I bid my reader farewell."

lessons.title: 15m Lit./Mythology: Grade 3 - Lesson 36
lessons.body:
Exam Week

➜ EXAMS 
Answer question(s) related to course.

Questions will come from:
Option 1: A Wonder Book for Girls and Boys
Option 2: Pilgrim's Progress Book 2

terms.content:
Lit./Mythology: Grade 3
· Pilgrim's Progress Book 2: Tell about or illustrate Christiana and Mercy’s experience at the wicket gate, OR tell about or illustrate the Interpreter’s teaching through the various rooms.

· A Wonder Book for Boys and Girls: Tell about or illustrate Hercules and the Old Man of the Sea, OR Tell about or illustrate Hercules meeting Atlas.

General Lit.: Grade 3
· Charlotte's Web: Tell about or illustrate one of the animals on the farm.

Lit./Mythology: Grade 3
· Pilgrim's Progress Book 2: Choose one to tell about or illustrate:
- The pilgrims' time spent in the Porter’s Lodge and its events
- Mr. Skill and Matthew’s illness.
- Traveling through the Valley of Humiliation and the stories told 
- Mr. Fearing and Mr. Gaius.

· A Wonder Book for Boys and Girls: Tell about or illustrate Baucis and Philemon serving a meal to the travelers, OR Tell all you can about or illustrate Quicksilver and his staff.

General Lit.: Grade 3
· Charlotte's Web: Tell about or illustrate one of your favorite parts of the book.

Lit./Mythology: Grade 3
· Pilgrim's Progress Book 2: Tell about or illustrate traveling through the Valley of Humiliation and the stories told, OR tell about or illustrate Mr. Fearing and Mr. Gaius.

· A Wonder Book for Boys and Girls: Tell about or illustrate Bellerophon’s search and discovery of Pegasus, OR Tell about or illustrate the battle against Chimaera.
